seo.title: Normalize — Turn messy files into clean data
seo.description: Upload a CSV, Excel, or JSON file. Normalize samples your data and suggests how to interpret it — column types, formats, and null tokens. You confirm and adjust per column, set output preferences, and download a clean dataset as CSV, JSON, or Parquet.
seo.ogTitle: Normalize — Turn messy files into clean data
seo.ogDescription: Upload a CSV, Excel, or JSON file. Normalize suggests how to interpret your data — you confirm, adjust per column, and export as CSV, JSON, or Parquet.

headline: Turn messy files into clean data.
subline: CSV, Excel, and JSON files rarely arrive clean. Normalize detects their structure, lets you confirm it, and produces a typed, validated dataset your pipeline can trust.

seo.title: New Upload — Normalize
seo.description: Start a new upload in Normalize. Bring a CSV, Excel, or JSON file into the review flow and prepare clean, typed exports.
seo.ogDescription: Upload a fresh CSV, Excel, or JSON file and move directly into Normalize's review and export workflow.

newPage.title: Bring in a fresh file and move straight into review.
newPage.description: Upload a CSV, Excel, or JSON file, confirm how every column should be interpreted, and prepare a clean export without scripts, manual cleanup, or guesswork.

review.label: Review first
review.title: Nothing gets normalized behind your back.
review.text: Every upload lands in the review flow first. You confirm column types, dates, separators, null tokens, and output choices before conversion starts.

artifacts.label: Outputs
artifacts.title: Each run produces more than a flat file.
artifacts.text: Completed runs include exports, quality metrics, parse issue visibility, and traceable artifacts so the result is easier to inspect and trust.

retention.label: Retention
retention.title: Built for active work, not long-term storage.
retention.text: Source files are removed after normalization, and generated output stays available only for a short download window.

summary.label: What to Expect
summary.title: One focused upload flow from raw file to usable dataset.
summary.text: This page is designed for a new upload only: choose a file, confirm the structure, and continue through the normalization flow without extra setup.

noAccount.title: No account required
noAccount.text: You can start with a file immediately and move into review without creating a profile.

singleFlow.title: Single-file workflow
singleFlow.text: Best for one fresh dataset at a time, especially when you want to inspect the structure before exporting.

exports.title: Export-ready result
exports.text: After review and normalization, you can download clean outputs in the formats supported by the workflow.

upload.title: Upload
upload.desc: Bring a CSV, Excel, or JSON file into Normalize.

confirm.title: Confirm structure
confirm.desc: Normalize samples the file and proposes how each column should be interpreted: types, date formats, null tokens, numeric separators. You confirm before any transformation runs.

output.title: Output settings
output.desc: Set your output format and normalization rules. Normalize applies them consistently across every row, resolving nulls, standardizing types, and recording every parse issue with its row and column.

download.title: Download
download.desc: Download the normalized dataset as CSV, Excel, JSON, or Parquet. Every output includes a quality report, a trace artifact, and a deterministic fingerprint.

scale.label: Scale
scale.text: Processes datasets up to 10 million rows. No batching, no scripts, no splitting files.

types.label: Type support
types.text: Every column gets a confirmed semantic type before conversion runs. Strings, booleans, dates, times, integers, decimals, currencies, percentages, fractions, signed values, and accounting notation.

artifacts.label: Output artifacts
artifacts.text: Every run produces a normalized dataset, a full trace artifact, and a manifest JSON, enough to audit, replay, or verify any output.

openSource.text: Normalize is open source. The engine, normalization rules, and pipeline are

waitlistEmail.subject: You're on the Normalize waitlist
waitlistEmail.heading: You're on the list.
waitlistEmail.p1: We'll email you directly when the hosted interface opens. No newsletter, no updates. Just the one email when it's ready.
waitlistEmail.p2: Normalize takes raw CSV, Excel, and JSON files and turns them into typed, validated datasets: confirmed structure, resolved nulls, and a deterministic output your pipeline can trust.
waitlistEmail.footer: You received this because you signed up at normalizeonline.com.
waitlistEmail.copyright: © {year} Normalize. Turn messy tabular files into clean, typed datasets.

uploadPad.heading: Drop a CSV, Excel, or JSON file here
uploadPad.legalNotice: By uploading, you agree to our <terms>Terms of Use</terms>, <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>, and <dataProtection>Data Protection</dataProtection>.
uploadPad.limitsNotice: Upload CSV up to {csvLimit} MB, Excel up to {xlsxLimit} MB, JSON up to {jsonLimit} MB.
uploadPad.redirecting: Taking you to the review page…
uploadPad.successDesc: Your file has been processed. Configure your columns to start normalization.

errors.unsupportedType: Unsupported file type. Use CSV, Excel (XLSX), or JSON.
errors.processFailed: Failed to process file. Please try again.
errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.

waitlist.heading: The hosted interface is in early access.
waitlist.description: Leave your email and we'll let you know when it opens, or explore the source on <githubLink>GitHub</githubLink> now.
waitlist.success: You're on the list. We'll be in touch.
